title: MANGA JManga Legal website to purchase Eva Manga
display_title: MANGA JManga Legal website to purchase Eva Manga
wikitext:
[]: [MANGA] JManga: Legal website to purchase Eva Manga             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Dec 07, 2011 10:02 pm August 2011 Excel Saga Forum's JManga information wrote: JManga , a new website where you can buy and read English-translated manga online "officially," launched last week. Currently, it's only available in North America, which apparently doesn't include Canada or - according to some reports - Georgia, but they plan to make their service available globally at some point. According to Anime News Network , one of the titles they plan to have for sale by 2013 is Excel Saga. Their prices are, frankly, terrible, but hey, if this place can finally make volumes 5, 7, and 8 available to a wider audience, then bring on the $9 downloads!

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Wed Dec 07, 2011 10:04 pm Seems Jmanga is only for America and Canada. Clicking the link only led to the following: "Thanks for coming to JManga! Unfortunately JManga is currently only open for users in United States of America and Canada, but don’t give up just yet, we are looking forward to spreading manga worldwide!! "

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Dec 07, 2011 10:26 pm There was an update to the ANN article here, for those curious: http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2011-08-24/jmanga-lists-more-new-titles (Hopefully JManga will fix their business model with time.)

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Dec 07, 2011 10:38 pm It won't happen unless they can secure distribution rights to other countries. It's not as much to do with their business model as the rights that they've licensed.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Dec 07, 2011 10:43 pm I meant regarding the pricing system. Whether or not they can release various manga internationally, yeah, it depends on distribution rights in other countries like you said.

[]:             Quote Post by Final Messenger » Thu Dec 08, 2011 9:21 am Odd that they don't have that 2 volume manga where Shinji was a detective on their site is it only for manga that had a offical release in the west?

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Thu Dec 08, 2011 11:26 am Man this is great, I can finally get volume 9,Come on 2013! @SSD Are you going to make this into a news story on the front page?

[]:             Quote Post by classified-zerogoki » Sat Dec 17, 2011 11:45 pm ^ FM, they really should translate Shinji Ikari's Detective Journal.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Dec 18, 2011 4:14 am ^ they really should translate Evangelion Anima

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Dec 18, 2011 11:45 am ^ I would have to second Tines-san's notion.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sun Dec 18, 2011 6:21 pm ^ Thirded.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Sun Dec 18, 2011 6:24 pm ^ Fourthed. This seems like a great idea, though it would be nice if they could reprint these books again as actual mangas. I mean, I stare at screens enough as it is, so I'm the kind of guy who prefers actual written-in-paper when in comes to reading and manga. My eyes need to relax once in a while.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Mon Dec 19, 2011 1:15 pm So, how cheaper is it than buying the books?
